channel.title: Inference — AI Newsletter
channel.description: The signal in the noise. Curated AI trends, tools, and research — updated every Monday.

item.title: Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government
item.description: Trump is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via Ars Technica AI).
item.author: Will Knight, WIRED.com

item.title: Interactive Demo: https://coasty.ai/share/a58ee8f6-3821-4795-b4e7-4d77679a6221
item.description: Worth a quick read — Interactive Demo tackles a gap most teams are still working around (via Dev.to AI).
item.author: Nitish Kovuru

item.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
item.description: Open distribution of IssueScout lowers adoption barriers and pressures proprietary alternatives (via Hacker News).

item.title: Letting Machines Decide What Matters
item.description: Concise coverage of Letting Machines Decide What Matters. Read it with your current stack in mind (via IEEE Spectrum AI).
item.author: Eliza Strickland

item.title: A Complete End-to-End Coding Guide to MLflow Experiment Tracking, Hyperparameter Optimization, Model Evaluation, and Live Model Deployment
item.description: Concrete enough to follow along — the End Coding Guide tutorial goes beyond toy examples (via MarkTechPost).
item.author: Michal Sutter

item.title: OpenAI and Amazon announce strategic partnership
item.description: The OpenAI announcement carries implications for budgets and vendor relationships (via OpenAI Blog).

item.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
item.description: OpenAI is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via TechCrunch AI).
item.author: Anthony Ha

item.title: How to Design a Production-Grade Multi-Agent Communication System Using LangGraph Structured Message Bus, ACP Logging, and Persistent Shared State Architecture
item.description: New findings around ACP may shape tooling choices once validated by independent teams (via MarkTechPost).
item.author: Asif Razzaq

item.title: Alibaba Team Open-Sources CoPaw: A High-Performance Personal Agent Workstation for Developers to Scale Multi-Channel AI Workflows and Memory
item.description: Open access to Alibaba Team Open gives teams a new self-hosted option to evaluate (via MarkTechPost).
item.author: Asif Razzaq

item.title: OpenClaw, but in containers: Meet NanoClaw
item.description: Concise coverage of OpenClaw. Read it with your current stack in mind (via The Register).
item.author: Thomas Claburn

item.title: SaaS-pocalypse chatter is doomster pr0n. It would be nice if enterprise IT were boring again
item.description: Worth a quick read — SaaS tackles a gap most teams are still working around (via The Register).
item.author: Lindsay Clark
